political.s: Stripe leans left politically with $141K in FEC donations (9% Republican, mostly Democratic contributions). The company supports climate initiatives through Stripe Climate and has engaged in charitable giving, reflecting progressive values.

charity.s: Stripe demonstrates strong charitable commitment through nonprofit pricing (2.2%+ fees) and processes $300M+ in donations on GivingTuesday alone. The company offers discounted processing for eligible 501(c)(3) organizations and partners with major nonprofits like the Red Cross.
charity.focus: Nonprofit payment processing, carbon removal, climate initiatives

environment.s: Stripe demonstrates strong environmental commitment through Stripe Climate program, offsetting company greenhouse gas emissions, and committing to pay for direct carbon dioxide removal. The company participates in carbon removal initiatives and allows customers to contribute to climate projects.

labor.s: Employee reviews show mixed sentiments with 3.7/5 rating on Glassdoor. Concerns include poor work-life balance (3.2/5), high workload, and staff turnover, though compensation is competitive at 4.3/5. January 2025 layoffs affecting ~300 workers raised concerns about job security.

dei.s: Stripe has a DEI rating of 3.7/5 on Glassdoor (matching industry average) and diversity score of 83/100 on Comparably, ranking in top 10% for similar-sized companies. The company maintains DEI program roles and initiatives, though some employee reviews question depth of commitment versus public statements.

animals.s: Not applicable for payment processing/fintech company

guns.s: Not applicable for payment processing/fintech company

privacy.s: Stripe maintains good privacy practices with PCI-DSS Level 1 certification (highest payment industry standard), end-to-end encryption, and multiple security frameworks. Complies with GDPR, CCPA, and maintains transparent privacy policies with data subject rights.

execPay.s: Executive compensation averages $214,847 annually, while overall average employee salary is $140,451. Based on available data, estimated pay ratio is approximately 1.5:1, suggesting relatively measured executive-to-worker pay gap compared to tech industry standards.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
